mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains at or near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 6% year-over-year, real rates turn deeply negative. Equity investors rationally shift capital away from cash and bonds into stocks as the only asset class offering positive real returns, driving S&P 500 forward returns positive over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-01-15, FEDFUNDS=0.08% and CPIAUCSL=280.192 (YoY ~7%), creating deeply negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 6%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% signals tight labor markets and strong wage growth expectations. Equity investors interpret this as evidence of robust consumer spending power and corporate earnings resilience, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.9% as of 2022-01-15, well below 4% threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%) when UNRATE < 4%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and foreshadows earnings downgrades. Equity investors reduce risk exposure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: demand slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances following RSAFS declines > 2% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while remaining positive, signaling supply-side weakness without outright contraction. Equity investors interpret this as a precursor to margin compression and reduced earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when INDPRO MoM growth is 0-0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, signaling synchronized demand strength across residential and consumption channels. This joint signal indicates broad-based economic momentum that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1679.0 thousand units, RSAFS=626833.0 million as of 2022-01-15
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS show required growth rates, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month while unemployment remains stable, signaling labor market cooling without immediate distress. Equity investors interpret this as the Fed's preferred soft-landing scenario, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession fears ease.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth 50k-100k AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
regime_hint: soft-landing scenario with moderating labor demand
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances when PAYEMS growth is 50k-100k with stable UNRATE, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate rises above 1.5% in a tightening cycle while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2% annualized, signaling policy error and recession risk. Equity investors reduce exposure to growth stocks, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal rate expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.08% and GDPC1=19478.893 billion (2017 dollars) as of 2022-01-15
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 1.5% AND GDPC1 growth < 2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contracts month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) remains flat or declines, signaling synchronized supply and labor weakness. This dual contraction indicates imminent recession, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity investors front-run earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM < 0% AND PAYEMS monthly change < 50k
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when INDPRO contracts MoM AND PAYEMS growth < 50k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
